nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.

nts1.n: Soup To Nuts w/ John Gómez
nts1.d: Collector John Gómez brightens up Mondays with some music from different times and different places.
From dusty crates and dingy basements to the NTS airwaves - reissue label Melodies International bring special, undiscovered, and beautiful records from across the world their show, every month.

Hip-hop and soul-inflected sounds from Richmond's Ohbliv.
From the host: "Ambient Jazz For The Mind and Spirit"

Edinburgh based party and reissue label present rarities and classics every month.
From the host:"Jazz Funk Classics and deepfunk rarities all the way this month, Euan Fryer Athens of the North, never safe, always a bit chaotic, that's the way I like it."
Welcome to Club Coco: a place where globe-trotting vinyl hunter Coco María shares her joyous discoveries - jazz, fusion, international pop, vintage latin music of all flavours…
Tune in to the sound of SUNCUT, boasting a heartfelt selection of some favourite 45’s through to classic bonafide soulful jams.
Dennis specialises in selections ranging from new wave electronics, lo-fi hip-hop, downtempo house and everything in-between. Also a member of the wider music and production group, Woodgrain Vicious.

Organ Tapes and friends play disparate music plied and wrenched together with religious fervour. Sound of E5 上海 monthly on a Monday.
From the host: "Keiska guestmix for Organ Tapes NTS"

Blueprints in sounds, stones to sparks, lead to gold, with Hong-Kong-born, Utrecht-based electronic musician gyrofield.
From the host: "winds thrash imaginary trees
everything else indoors with me
austere fitting of a tory sort of god
he happens upon my thrifted rug
the north star is more like a sodium lamp
whose sunrise fades into noisy nothingness
through the black of my shadow
this god smiles on me"

nts1.n: Music For Days Like These w/ John Talabot (R)
The Head Zone w/ Ripley Johnson (R)
THE EARLY BIRD SHOW w/ MARTHA SKYE MURPHY
The NTS Breakfast Show w/ Flo
CHANNELING W/ IVAN SMAGGHE & NATHAN GREGORY WILKINS

nts2.d: Avant-garde musician and composer Jim O'Rourke pulls stops by once a month, pulling an hour of picks from his extensive record collection.
Underground legend Bloodz Boi leaves untold emotions in sounds. Broadcast from Beijing on a Tuesday every month.

nts2.n: CORIN: MANTRA OF LIFE W/ Rani Jambak
nts2.d:
Around the globe and across the spectrum of electronic music with DJ and producer CORIN. A monthly hour of warped percussion and ambient explorations.
From the host: "Though life may seem to stand on the brink of ruin, beauty still lingers in the hopes and prayers whispered with sincerity. The mantra of life unfolds in the symbols of human encounters with nature and the supernatural, reminding us that, in the end, we are but a small part of the vast universe."

nts2.n: PAVEL MILYAKOV: Feuerwerk W/ Timothy George Kelly, Dylan Spencer-Davidson
nts2.d:
Berlin-based artist Pavel Milyakov aka Buttechno joins us for an hour a month.
From the host: "Music from the universe of the Berlin short film FEUERWERK. FEUERWERK is a short documentary about Berlin, New Year’s Eve, and catharsis. This mix was created by the film’s director, Timothy George Kelly in collaboration with Dylan Spencer-Davidson."
Henriette and Catharina of Smerz take over the NTS airwaves once a month with a selection of classical, folk, ambient, pop and more.

Los Angeles producer & sound artist claire rousay journeys through healing plaintive electronics, quotidian field recordings & minimalist compositions.
From the host: "an hour of casual piano, human speech, and found sound"

Relaxing & spiritual sounds with Copenhagen-based composer & producer Sofie Birch.
From the host: "Spring is here, flowers are coming up making space for longing and lightness at the same time."

nts2.n: COMO LA FLOR W/ JAZMIN - PURO SENTIMENTAL
nts2.d:
Música sentimental for all the sad girls and sad boys.
From the host: "SOÑANDO DE UN MUNDO DONDE TODOS TIENEN EL DERECHO DE VIVIR EN PAZ."
Sarah Davachi serenades the LA airwaves once a month with Le Jardin - an amalgamation of progressive rock, acid folk, early music, experimental productions, & minimal sympathetic sounds.

nts2.n: Day Care w/ DJ Randy Ellis, The Alaia, & Jeff Nelson: Drum and Bass from '93-'06
nts2.d: CQQL Records founders DJ Randy Ellis and The Alaia bring you Day Care, a 2-hour monthly afternoon curriculum of Boogie Funk, Disco, R&B, G-Funk, House, Modern Soul wax, and way way more. Expect to be powdered and pampered by these audio obsessives.
nts2.j: DJ Randy Ellis, The Alaia, & Jeff Nelson

nts2.n: Hora de la flor w/ LAFLOR
nts2.d: Colombian-Miamian vinyl collector and DJ – LAFLOR presents the sounds of her roots, playing an hour of salsa romántica records.

nts2.n: What Means The World 2 U w/ Michael David & Ron Snake
El Sabor De La Ciudad w/ REGAL86 (R)
NTS Guide to: Giorno Poetry Systems (R)
YOU'LL SOON KNOW W/ TIM PARKER

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

dublab.n: Mike Harding and Bana Haffar - Long Wave
Echo Park Film Center - Optical Track
The Urxed - Seeing It As You Really Are

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Hannah Rubin and Noelle Armstrong – MELLOW DRAMA
Frankie Jax No Mad - Not Evil Radio
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Ryan Lee Moore – Holy Ghost Honky Tonk
Nia Michelle, Jordan Collins, Lewis Thompson – Channels
